title: Dry Delicate Greek Eggplant Casserole
description: A bitter, molten, lazy-sunday Greek dish featuring orange juice, perfect for a vegetarian-friendly meal. This casserole is a unique blend of flavors and textures.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the orange juice, tomato paste, garlic, oregano, salt, and pepper.
Add the sliced eggplant to the bowl and toss to coat with the orange juice mixture.
Let the eggplant slices sit for 10 minutes to allow them to absorb the flavors.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the eggplant slices until they are golden brown.
In a separate bowl, mix together the crumbled feta cheese and pitted kalamata olives.
In a 9x13 inch baking dish, create a layer of eggplant slices.
Top the eggplant layer with a layer of the feta cheese and olive mixture.
Repeat the layers until all the ingredients have been used, ending with a layer of eggplant on top.
Bake the casserole in the preheated oven for 30 minutes, or until the eggplant is tender and the top is golden brown.
